15

Input

Q: How many layers should we use?
вАҐ Theoretical answer:
вАУ A neural network with 1 hidden layer is a universal function 
approximator
вАУ Cybenko (1989): For any continuous function g(x), there 
exists a 1-hidden-layer neural net hќЄ(x) 
s.t. | hќЄ(x) вАУ g(x) | < ѕµ for all x, assuming sigmoid activation 
functions
вАҐ Empirical answer:
вАУ Before 2006: вАЬDeep networks (e.g. 3 or more hidden layers) 
are too hard to trainвАЭ
вАУ After 2006: вАЬDeep networks are easier to train than shallow 
networks (e.g. 2 or fewer layers) for many problemsвАЭ
Big caveat: You need to know and use the right tricks.
